claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

First, could you tell me: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children do you have, and what are their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 6 years old and he lives with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Are you or your partner responsible for Alex?** By this I mean, do you (or your partner) look after Alex and are responsible for his upbringing, such as providing for his care, food, clothing, and general welfare?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex. I look after him and take care of all his needs - his food, clothing, and everything else he needs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Do you normally live in the UK?** And is Alex also normally living in the UK with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives here with me as well.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I need to check a few more details to complete the assessment.

**Are you currently receiving any other benefits or payments for Alex, such as:**
- Child Benefit from another country (like an EU member state or another country)?
- Any equivalent child-related benefits or allowances from abroad?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any child benefits or payments from another country for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions:

**Does anyone else also claim to be responsible for Alex, or is there anyone else who might be claiming Child Benefit for him?** For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nobody else claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:**
- In local authority care (care home or with foster parents)?
- In hospital or a residential care facility for an extended period?
- Living with someone else who is not you (even temporarily)?

Or does Alex live with you full-time in your household?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually Alex has been in local authority care for a short while - about 7 weeks or so. But he does live with me, he's not there full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is important information. Let me ask a follow-up question:

**When you say Alex has been in local authority care for about 7 weeks, do you mean:**
- He is currently in care now and has been there for 7 weeks continuously?
- Or he was in care for 7 weeks but is now back living with you full-time?

Also, if he is currently in care, does he come home to you on some days, or does he stay in care all the time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He is currently in care and has been there for 7 weeks. He doesn't come home to stay with me - he's there full-time at the care home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is an important detail for your eligibility. Let me ask one more question to complete the assessment:

**Do you continue to contribute to the costs of providing for Alex while he is in local authority care?** For example, do you pay towards his clothing, personal items, or other costs for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I haven't been able to contribute financially towards his costs while he's been in care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result

Based on the information you've provided, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Here's why:

While you are responsible for Alex and he is under 16 (which would normally meet the basic requirements), there's an important rule about children in local authority care:

**When a child is in local authority care full-time**, you can only continue to receive Child Benefit if you are **contributing to the costs of providing for the child** (such as paying towards clothing, personal items, or other costs). 

Since Alex has been in care full-time for 7 weeks and you haven't been able to contribute financially towards his costs during this time, you cannot claim Child Benefit for him while he remains in this situation.

### Important to know:

- If Alex returns to live with you full-time, you would then be able to claim Child Benefit for him again
- If you start contributing to his costs while he's in care, this may affect your eligibility

If your circumstances change, you may wish to reassess your eligibility at that time. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16) and you are responsible for him. However, Alex has been in local authority care full-time for 7 weeks continuously. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can only continue to receive Child Benefit for a child in local authority care for up to 8 weeks (56 days) if you continue to contribute to the costs of providing for the child. Since you have not been contributing financially towards Alex's costs while he has been in care, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex during this period when he is in local authority care.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for their 6-year-old son Alex and lives in the UK, which would normally satisfy the basic eligibility criteria. However, Alex is currently in local authority care full-time and has been for 7 weeks. The claimant has not been contributing to the costs of providing for Alex while he is in care. Under Child Benefit rules, when a child is in local authority care, the claimant must be contributing to the child's costs to remain eligible for Child Benefit during that period. Since this condition is not met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex while he remains in care without the claimant contributing to his costs.

According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can only continue to receive Child Benefit for a child in local authority care for up to 8 weeks (56 days) if you continue to contribute to the costs of providing for the child. Since you have not been contributing financially towards Alex's costs while he has been in care, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex during this period when he is in local authority care.

However, Alex is currently in local authority care full-time and has been for 7 weeks. The claimant has not been contributing to the costs of providing for Alex while he is in care. Under Child Benefit rules, when a child is in local authority care, the claimant must be contributing to the child's costs to remain eligible for Child Benefit during that period. Since this condition is not met, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex while he remains in care without the claimant contributing to his costs.
